marketState: neutral, leaning constructive: bullish weather + extreme short positioning offset by a storage surplus and bearish weekly trend
summary: NG front month ($2.955) is essentially at fair value ($2.919 fair, $2.42-$3.42 band) and only modestly below its 5-year median context (-3.6pp), so there is no clean statistical mispricing to trade right now. The standout signal is the 8-16 day weather window: cooling demand is running +38% vs the 5-year norm and above the historical max range, with the demand trend up +18.2 in 24h to +20% vs normal — a real bullish catalyst for late-summer gas burn that the market has not obviously priced in yet. Managed money is net short 105,709 contracts, at just the 8th percentile of 5-year positioning, an extreme that has historically preceded short-covering rallies. Countering this, storage sits +183 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +17 Bcf build surprise, and the daily/weekly technical trend is still bearish (RSI 38.7-43, negative MACD) even though intraday timeframes (5m/15m) have flipped neutral-to-bullish. On balance the multi-week case is cautiously bullish (weather + COT extreme vs. storage overhang), while the 1-10 day picture is too mixed for a high-conviction directional trade.

longTerm.entry: $2.85 - $2.95 (scale in on any pullback toward fair value)
longTerm.rationale: 8-16D cooling demand is +38% vs 5y and above the historical max range with a sharply rising trend, seasonality favors Sep strength (69% odds higher in 4wks, typical +3.7% move) and managed money is at an 8th-percentile net-short extreme that skews toward mean-reversion higher; storage's +183 Bcf surplus and bearish build surprise are the main offsetting risk, which is why conviction is medium rather than high.
longTerm.invalidation: A confirmed break and close below $2.65, a further deterioration in the 8-16D weather outlook back toward/below the 5y norm, or another materially bearish storage surprise that pushes the 5y surplus wider

shortTerm.rationale: Near-term weather (demand_1_7D) is neutral (0% vs 5y, mid-range), price is already FAIR on both the now and +7D fair-value checks (no discount to buy), and technicals are split — intraday (5m/15m/1h) neutral-to-bullish vs daily/weekly still bearish — so there is no clean short-horizon edge yet despite the constructive medium-term backdrop.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) daily trend/RSI to turn up (1d RSI clears ~50 with a bullish EMA cross) confirming the intraday bounce is carrying through, or (b) a pullback into the $2.80-$2.85 zone that lines up with the longer-term long, or (c) the 8-16D heat signal rolling into the 1-7D window with pctVs5y turning clearly positive

Managed money net short 105,709 contracts at the 8th percentile of 5y positioning - extreme, short-covering risk

risks: Seasonality reliability is rated Low (63pp 10-90% spread this week) - limited confidence in the seasonal edge
